question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker who obtains a valid EcrecoverAuthorizer signature from a borrower use the resulting coarse-grained authorization to call supplyCollateral(market, i, 1, borrower) for a collateral index whose oracle reverts, setting bit i in collateralBitmap and causing every subsequent isHealthy, withdrawCollateral, and take (when seller has debt) call for that borrower to revert, under preconditions: borrower has authorized EcrecoverAuthorizer on Midnight, attacker holds a valid Authorization signature with isAuthorized=true, market has a collateral index with a reverting oracle, borrower has nonzero debt; call sequence: ecrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized(auth, sig) -> supplyCollateral(market, poisonIndex, 1, borrower); violating invariant: a bit in collateralBitmap must be set if and only if position.collateral[i] != 0, and activated-collateral oracle revert must not be triggerable by unauthorized parties; causing scoped impact: bitmap includes an active-but-reverting-oracle collateral, health checks and liquidation loops revert, borrower position is frozen? Proof idea: fuzz collateralIndex in [0, market.collateralParams.length), set oracle to reverting mock, assert isHealthy reverts and withdrawCollateral reverts after poisoning.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / withdrawCollateral / supplyCollateral via multicall] Can an authorized operator (authorization granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) use multicall to call withdrawCollateral(market, i, fullAmount, borrower, operator) then supplyCollateral(market, i, 0, borrower) in the same transaction, under preconditions: borrower has nonzero collateral[i] and bit i set, operator is authorized, assets=0 in supplyCollateral; call sequence: multicall([withdrawCollateral(i, fullAmount), supplyCollateral(i, 0)]); violating invariant: collateral[i] == 0 after full withdrawal but bit i must be cleared; causing scoped impact: if supplyCollateral with assets=0 does not set the bit (correct) but a subsequent re-supply path is blocked, does the bitmap remain consistent? Proof idea: unit test asserting collateralBitmap bit i == 0 after full withdrawal and that supplyCollateral(i, 0) is a no-op on the bitmap.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / withdrawCollateral interleaved] Can an authorized operator interleave supplyCollateral and withdrawCollateral calls across MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) indices to exhaust the bitmap cap, then supply to a new index, under preconditions: borrower has 15 active collaterals, operator is authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer, market has 17+ collateral params; call sequence: supplyCollateral(market, 15, 1, borrower) -> expect TooManyActivatedCollaterals revert; violating invariant: countBits(collateralBitmap) <= MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER; causing scoped impact: if the cap check is bypassable, the while-loop in isHealthy and liquidate iterates beyond 16 times, potentially causing gas exhaustion or incorrect maxDebt? Proof idea: fuzz numCollaterals in [17, 128], assert TooManyActivatedCollaterals is always thrown at the 17th activation.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / liquidate (seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0)] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate(market, collateralIndex, 0, 0, borrower, false, ...) on a collateral index that is NOT in the bitmap (bit not set, collateral[i]=0), under preconditions: borrower is unhealthy, collateralIndex is a valid market index but not activated; call sequence: liquidate(market, inactiveIndex, 0, 0, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), ''); violating invariant: the bitmap loop computes maxDebt and badDebt only over activated collaterals; causing scoped impact: bad debt is realized using only the bitmap-iterated collaterals, but the seize/repay block is skipped, leaving the bitmap unchanged while debt is reduced — does this create a desync between debt and collateral accounting? Proof idea: assert that after a (0,0) liquidation on an inactive index, isHealthy(market, id, borrower) == isHealthyNoBitmap(market, id, borrower).

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / liquidate callback reentrancy] Can a liquidator's callback (ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate) re-enter supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, amount, borrower) after the bitmap bit was cleared (newCollateral==0 after seizure), under preconditions: liquidator passes a malicious callback contract, collateralIndex is fully seized (newCollateral=0), callback re-enters supplyCollateral for the same index; call sequence: liquidate(market, i, fullCollateral, 0, borrower, false, receiver, maliciousCallback, data) -> onLiquidate re-enters supplyCollateral(market, i, amount, borrower); violating invariant: after liquidate clears bit i, re-entering supplyCollateral sets bit i again with new collateral — does the post-liquidation health check in take (line 476) or subsequent isHealthy calls correctly reflect the re-activated collateral? Proof idea: assert collateralBitmap bit i is set after callback re-supply and collateral[i] == amount.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / take sell callback / supplyCollateral] Can a sell-side taker callback (ISellCallback.onSell) call supplyCollateral(market, i, amount, seller) for a collateral index not previously activated, under preconditions: seller has debt, bit i is not set, callback is the taker's callback; call sequence: take(offer, ..., takerCallback, data) -> onSell re-enters supplyCollateral(market, i, amount, seller); violating invariant: the health check at line 476 (isHealthy after sell callback) uses the bitmap state at the time of the check — if the callback sets a new bit, does isHealthy correctly include the new collateral? Proof idea: assert isHealthy returns true after callback-supplied collateral is included in the bitmap.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / withdrawCollateral with assets=0] Can an authorized operator call withdrawCollateral(market, i, 0, borrower, receiver) when collateral[i] > 0, under preconditions: bit i is set, collateral[i] > 0, assets=0; call sequence: withdrawCollateral(market, i, 0, borrower, receiver); violating invariant: newCollateral = collateral[i] - 0 = collateral[i] > 0, so the clearBit condition (newCollateral==0 && assets>0) is false — bitmap unchanged, correct; but does isHealthy still pass? Proof idea: assert that withdrawCollateral(i, 0) is a no-op on both collateral[i] and the bitmap, and that isHealthy returns the same value before and after.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral with assets=0] Can an authorized operator call supplyCollateral(market, i, 0, borrower) when collateral[i] == 0, under preconditions: bit i is not set, collateral[i] == 0, assets=0; call sequence: supplyCollateral(market, i, 0, borrower); violating invariant: oldCollateral=0 and assets=0, so the setBit condition (oldCollateral==0 && assets>0) is false — bitmap unchanged; but collateral[i] = toUint128(0+0) = 0 — no change; does this create any state inconsistency? Proof idea: assert that supplyCollateral(i, 0) is a strict no-op on both collateral[i] and the bitmap.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / multicall: supplyCollateral then withdrawCollateral same index same tx] Can an authorized operator use multicall to call supplyCollateral(market, i, amount, borrower) then withdrawCollateral(market, i, amount, borrower, receiver) in the same transaction, under preconditions: collateral[i] == 0 before multicall, borrower has debt; call sequence: multicall([supplyCollateral(i, amount), withdrawCollateral(i, amount)]); violating invariant: after the multicall, collateral[i] == 0 and bit i == 0 — but the health check in withdrawCollateral uses the bitmap state after the supply (bit i set) — does the health check correctly include the just-supplied-then-withdrawn collateral? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / isHealthy vs isHealthyNoBitmap equivalence] Can any sequence of authorized operations (via EcrecoverAuthorizer) produce a state where isHealthy(market, id, borrower) != isHealthyNoBitmap(market, id, borrower), under preconditions: borrower has debt, market has N collateral params, authorized operator performs arbitrary supplyCollateral/withdrawCollateral/liquidate calls; call sequence: arbitrary authorized operations; violating invariant: nonZeroCollateralsAreActivated — collateral[i] != 0 <=> bit i set; causing scoped impact: if the invariant is broken, isHealthy and isHealthyNoBitmap diverge, enabling healthy-position liquidation or unhealthy-position protection? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / liquidate normal mode with bitmap-skipped collateral] Can a liquidator liquidate a borrower whose highest-value collateral has its bitmap bit NOT set (due to prior corruption), causing the liquidation loop to compute a lower maxDebt than the true value, under preconditions: collateral[highValueIndex] > 0 but bit highValueIndex is not set (hypothetical corruption), borrower's true maxDebt >= debt but bitmap-computed maxDebt < debt; call sequence: liquidate(market, anyIndex, seizedAssets, 0, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), ''); violating invariant: only unhealthy accounts should be liquidatable; causing scoped impact: healthy borrower is liquidated, losing collateral without being undercollateralized? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / authorized operator activates collateral with high-price oracle to inflate maxDebt] Can an authorized operator supply a tiny amount of a high-price collateral to inflate the borrower's maxDebt, allowing the borrower to borrow more than their true collateral value supports, under preconditions: operator is authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer, market has a collateral with oracle returning near-max price, borrower has existing debt; call sequence: supplyCollateral(market, highPriceIndex, 1, borrower) -> take(sellOffer, ...) to increase debt; violating invariant: every debt increase must pass collateral and gate checks; causing scoped impact: borrower borrows against 1-wei collateral valued at near-max price, creating bad debt if oracle price drops? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / signature replay across EcrecoverAuthorizer instances] Can an attacker replay a valid Authorization signature on a different EcrecoverAuthorizer instance (different address, same MIDNIGHT), under preconditions: two EcrecoverAuthorizer contracts deployed pointing to the same Midnight, borrower has signed an authorization for instance A; call sequence: ecrecoverAuthorizerB.setIsAuthorized(auth, sig) where auth was signed for instance A; violating invariant: the EIP-712 domain separator includes address(this) (the EcrecoverAuthorizer address), so the digest differs — replay should fail; causing scoped impact: if domain separation is insufficient, attacker gains unauthorized authorization, enabling bitmap manipulation? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / nonce race condition] Can two transactions using the same nonce race to both succeed if submitted in the same block, under preconditions: borrower has nonce N, two different callers submit setIsAuthorized with nonce N simultaneously; call sequence: tx1: ecrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized(auth1_nonce_N, sig1) and tx2: ecrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized(auth2_nonce_N, sig2) in same block; violating invariant: nonce[authorizer] increments atomically, only one can succeed; causing scoped impact: if both succeed, the second authorization is applied with a stale nonce, potentially granting unintended authorization that enables bitmap manipulation? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized / liquidate bitmap loop vs isHealthy bitmap loop divergence] Can the liquidate bitmap loop (lines 607-618) and the isHealthy bitmap loop (lines 950-957) produce different results for the same position state, under preconditions: borrower has multiple active collaterals with varying prices; call sequence: liquidate(market, i, 0, 0, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '') to realize bad debt; violating invariant: both loops must iterate over exactly the same set of active collaterals; causing scoped impact: if the loops diverge (e.g., due to a bitmap mutation between the two calls), maxDebt in liquidate differs from maxDebt in isHealthy, enabling liquidation of a healthy position?
